attempts.strategy: Verbatim probe-confirmed body from PROGRESS.md template — single-field Prop-class with one existential field universally quantified over affine opens U, asserting existence of (ι : Type u, 𝒰 : ι → Opens, ⨆𝒰 = U, IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰, HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰).
attempts.code_tried:
class HasAffineCechAcyclicCover
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    Prop where
  exists_cover : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
    IsAffineOpen U →
      ∃ (ι : Type u) (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat),
        (⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U) ∧ IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰 ∧ HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰
attempts.insight: Plain Prop-class with one ∀-quantified existential field. The ∃-and-∧ packaging mirrors iter-040 / iter-043 / iter-048 / iter-050 carrier-predicate decomposition: each iteration introduces one carrier, defers its instantiation, is consumed downstream via instance synthesis. Universe-`u` annotation on `ι` is required so `𝒰 : ι → Opens (...).left.toTopCat` lives in the same universe as the rest of the consumer chain. No new axiom introduced — kernel-only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound] (verified review-side via lean_verify).

next_steps: iter-054+ will instantiate `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` via the substantive Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem and basic-open Koszul acyclicity producers.

attempts.strategy: Verbatim probe-confirmed tactic body from PROGRESS.md template — `obtain` destructure of the carrier's existential, two `haveI` lines registering the conjuncts as local class instances, then chain iter-052's generalised rewrite-bridge consumer.
attempts.code_tried:
instance instIsAffineHModuleVanishing_of_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F] : IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F where
  subsingleton_HModule' {U} hU i hi := by
    obtain ⟨ι, 𝒰, hcov, hacyc, hcomp⟩ :=
      HasAffineCechAcyclicCover.exists_cover (F := F) hU
    haveI : IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰 := hacyc
    haveI : HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰 := hcomp
    exact subsingleton_HModule'_of_hasCechToHModuleIso hcov i hi
attempts.insight: The two `haveI` lines are load-bearing: they convert the ∃-conjuncts (regular hypotheses) into local class instances so iter-052's consumer (which takes them as `[…]` arguments) fires via instance synthesis. The `(F := F)` named-argument is required when calling `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover.exists_cover`, since `F` cannot be inferred from `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` alone. Marked as `instance` (not `theorem`) so `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F` synthesizes automatically downstream once any `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` instance is in scope. Single-universe `[HasExt.{u}]` annotation only — no iter-034 universe bridge needed (mirrors iter-052's single-universe constraint). Kernel-only axioms verified review-side via lean_verify.

next_steps: Compose with iter-053+ basic-open Koszul producer + Čech-vs-derived comparison to instantiate `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` and supply `[IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)]` automatically to iter-040's consumer chain, unblocking Phase A step 6.
